notes:
# Spec: brainstorm-bead scope expansion (AC classification, policy knobs, human follow-ups)

## Summary

Expand the brainstorm-bead formula so that the brainstorm phase captures
post-implementation policy decisions: which acceptance criteria are
machine-verifiable vs. require human judgment, whether the bead should
auto-merge, and what depth of code review applies. Wire the downstream
formulas (merge-and-cleanup, implement-feature, fix-bug) to honor those
decisions.

## Background / Motivation

Today the brainstorm phase captures *what to build* but not *what
happens after it's built*. Acceptance criteria are written as if all
items are mechanically checkable, no per-bead policy is captured for
review depth or merge auth, and any human verification work gets
discovered late (or never). Downstream formulas then have to make
those policy calls ad-hoc — or they get skipped.

This bead pushes the policy decisions back to brainstorm time, where
the human is already engaged and the spec context is fresh. The
`bead-spec` agent (created by 7bk.3) does the cognitive work of
proposing classifications and knob values; the user confirms or
overrides.

## Requirements

### R1 — AC classification

- AC lines in `--acceptance` are tagged `[m]` (mechanical) or `[h]`
  (human). Tags are case-sensitive, must appear at the start of each
  line, followed by exactly one ASCII space.
- **Storage model**: `bd update --acceptance "<string>"` REPLACES the
  acceptance_criteria field with a single string. Multi-line content
  is stored verbatim with embedded `\n` newlines (verified by probe).
  An "AC line" is one `\n`-separated portion of that string. The
  `classify-and-policy` step composes the full multi-line string and
  passes it in a single `bd update --acceptance` call.
- **Canonical line parser** (every consumer inlines this; ~5 lines of
  regex/awk/jq depending on language):

  ```
  Pattern:   ^\[(m|h)\]\s(.*)$
  Match:     group 1 = tag (m|h); group 2 = AC text
  No match:  treat as untagged → default to [m]
  ```

  Apply per-line after splitting the field on `\n`. Trim trailing
  whitespace per line. Skip blank lines.
- **Backwards compat / collision avoidance**: lines beginning with `[`
  but NOT matching `^\[(m|h)\]\s` (e.g. `[BUG] foo`, `[FIX] bar`,
  `[ ] todo`, `[x] done`, `[Mechanical] foo`) are treated as
  **untagged** and default to `[m]`. The strict regex (case-sensitive,
  single character, requires trailing space) prevents both
  misclassification and silent drops.
- The `bead-spec` agent drafts AC tags during `write-spec` based on
  whether each item is testable (unit/integration/build/lint/type) or
  requires human judgment (visual layout, UX feel, content quality,
  semantic correctness of generated text, etc.). User reviews and may
  override any tag.

### R2 — Policy knobs (labels on the source bead)

Two cross-cutting policy knobs, stored as bd labels:

- **`auto-mergeable`** — presence label, no value. When present,
  `merge-and-cleanup` skips the explicit human "go ahead and merge"
  authorization gate. Default: absent (false).

  **Operational definition of "clean"** — auto-merge fires only when
  ALL of the following hold immediately before the merge call:
  1. Zero open Copilot review threads on the PR.
  2. Zero open human-author review threads on the PR.
  3. CI checks all green (no pending, no failed).
  4. Quiet window: no new comments or review activity on the PR
     within the last 5 minutes (configurable via the formula's
     description; default 5).

  **On wait-for-pr-comments timeout**: timeout is treated as NOT
  clean. Require human merge auth. Safer default — better to ask than
  ship unreviewed.

  **Re-check immediately before merge**: the merge step re-runs the
  cleanliness check after acquiring the merge intent, to catch
  comments that arrived between the last review cycle and merge time.

- **`review-level:<value>`** — key:value label, exactly one per bead.
  `bd` accepts `:` in label names (verified by probe). Values:
  - `none` — skip code-review, simplify, and verify entirely.
    Reserved for things like .gitignore additions where review is
    theatre.
  - `light` — verifier only (build/test/lint/type). No code-review or
    simplify pass. For typo fixes, doc updates, dependency bumps.
  - `standard` — current full gate: code-reviewer → simplify →
    verify. The default when no `review-level:*` label is present.
  - `deep` — standard gate + adversarial Codex pass auto-invoked.
    For architecture-sensitive, security-touching, or final pre-merge
    work.

  Default: `review-level:standard` implicit when no `review-level:*`
  label is present.

### R3 — Human follow-up beads

For every `[h]`-tagged AC line on the source bead, the `finalize` step
spawns one child bead:

- `--parent <source-bead-id>` — child of source bead
- `--type task`
- `--title "[Human verify] <AC text minus [m]/[h] tag>"`
- `--description` — verbatim AC text plus a one-line warning:
  `"WARNING: closing this bead without verifying allows the source
  bead to merge. Re-verify before closing."`
- `--priority` inherited from source
- `--assignee` inherited from source
- Labels: `human` (so the bead appears in `bd human list`)
- NOT labeled `implementation-ready` — `run-queue` ignores it.

**Human follow-ups are leaf tasks**: they themselves are not eligible
for brainstorming, are not assigned `[m]`/`[h]` AC of their own, and
do not spawn further follow-ups. Their AC is the verify action itself.

**Source bead waits via gate step**:

- `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` adds a **gate step** that depends
  on the source bead being closed before the merge step proceeds.
  The source bead's I2 close-walk closes it only when all children
  (the human follow-ups) close. So merge-and-cleanup's gate clears
  when the human follow-ups close.
- The formula uses `bd mol ready --gated` semantics: the gate step
  registers the source bead as its blocker; when the source bead
  closes, `bd mol ready --gated` surfaces the molecule for an agent
  to resume. No polling loop, no errored steps, clean resume model.
- If no `[h]` AC was tagged on the source bead, the source bead has
  no human follow-up children; the implement formula closes it at
  merge time as today, and the gate clears immediately.

**Epic close-walk participates normally**: the source bead's eventual
closure runs the standard I2 walk up to its parent epic; no special
handling for the epic chain. Human-verified beads behave identically
to all-mechanical beads in the cascade.

### R4 — brainstorm-bead.formula.toml changes

- **`write-spec`** step description updated: instruct the agent to
  draft AC with `[m]`/`[h]` tags, defaulting untagged to `[m]`.
  Document the canonical line parser from R1.
- **NEW `classify-and-policy`** step inserted between `write-spec` and
  `ralf-spec-review`:
  - Re-reads AC tags; challenges any tag that looks miscategorized
    ("you tagged 'response time < 200ms' as `[h]` — that's
    machine-measurable, recommend `[m]`").
  - Proposes `auto-mergeable: true|false` with a one-sentence
    rationale based on bead scope.
  - Proposes `review-level:<value>` with a one-sentence rationale.
  - Presents all three recommendations together; single user
    confirmation cycle; user accepts/overrides each.
  - Stamps the agreed labels: `bd label remove` for any existing
    `review-level:*` first, then `bd label add` for the chosen value;
    `bd label add auto-mergeable` if true.
  - Carries `Agent: bead-spec` directive (depends on 7bk.3 having
    landed).
- **`ralf-spec-review`** scope widened: heuristically reviews
  classification + knob choices for plausibility (e.g., flag
  `review-level:none` on a bead whose title/spec mentions
  `auth`/`token`/`password`/`permission`). Best-effort heuristic on
  spec text — final call rests with the user.
- **`finalize`** description extended: spawn human follow-up child
  beads per R3 before stamping `implementation-ready`.

### R5 — Consumer formula changes

- **`merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml`**:
  - Add a **gate step** before merge that blocks on the source bead
    being closed. The gate clears when the source bead closes (which
    happens when all `[h]` follow-up children close, via I2 walk).
    `bd mol ready --gated` surfaces the molecule for resume when the
    gate clears.
  - Add a `check-cleanliness` step before merge that evaluates the
    R2 "clean" definition.
  - If `auto-mergeable` label is present AND check-cleanliness
    returns clean, skip the human auth gate and proceed to merge
    (with the immediate re-check from R2).
  - If `auto-mergeable` is absent OR check-cleanliness returns dirty,
    require explicit human auth as today.
- **`implement-feature.formula.toml`**:
  - Each gate step (code-review, simplify, verify, adversarial-codex)
    self-skips based on the source bead's `review-level:*` label.
    The step description carries the skip rule; the dispatched agent
    reads the label first and exits early if not applicable. Pattern
    (c): step description carries the conditional, no new formula
    primitive.
  - Skip matrix:
    - `none` → code-review skips, simplify skips, verify skips
    - `light` → code-review skips, simplify skips, verify runs
    - `standard` → code-review runs, simplify runs, verify runs
      (current behavior); adversarial-codex skips
    - `deep` → code-review runs, simplify runs, verify runs,
      adversarial-codex runs
  - The adversarial-codex step is NEW — only present in the
    formula's step list when one might want it; under `deep` it
    runs, otherwise it self-skips.
- **`fix-bug.formula.toml`** — same `review-level:*` self-skip
  gating pattern applied to its review/simplify/verify steps.

### R6 — Documentation updates

- `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`:
  - Bead Lifecycle and Labels table extended with `auto-mergeable`
    and `review-level:*` rows (set by, meaning, who reads).
  - Note that `[h]`-spawned human follow-ups are children of the
    source bead and participate in the I2 close walk.
  - Cross-reference the canonical AC line parser in R1.
- `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/delivery.md`:
  - Note the `auto-mergeable` short-circuit (with the operational
    "clean" definition) and the gate-step mechanism that blocks
    merge on open `[h]` follow-up children.

## Out of Scope

- Beads with **only** `[h]` AC (spec-only, no implementation work).
  Defer to a follow-up bead if needed.
- AC tag aliases (`[mechanical]`, `[H]`, `[M]`, `[Mechanical]`).
  Strict regex `^\[(m|h)\]\s` only.
- Migration of existing in-flight beads. The implicit defaults
  (`[m]` untagged, `auto-mergeable: false`, `review-level:standard`)
  handle them transparently.
- A shared AC-parsing library. Each consumer inlines the canonical
  pattern (R1).
- A new formula primitive for conditional steps. Pattern (c) — step
  self-skips by reading its source bead's labels — is sufficient.
- Recursive human follow-ups. Human follow-ups are leaf tasks per R3.

## Design Notes

- **Why labels not notes for the knobs**: labels are the existing
  idiom for cross-cutting tags (`for-bead-*`, `implementation-ready`,
  `human`). They're queryable via `bd ready --label`, additive, and
  visible in `bd label list`. Notes would bury the policy.
- **Why agent recommends, not prompts cold**: the user already engaged
  in brainstorming has fresh context but limited bandwidth for "from
  scratch" decisions. Agent does the heavy lifting; user has final
  say. Same pattern as the spec itself — agent drafts, user reviews.
- **Why follow-ups are children of source, not `discovered-from`**:
  the semantics of "this bead is not done until humans verify too"
  require the source bead to wait. C2 (decision in Q7) — child
  placement + merge-and-cleanup gate step — enforces that
  mechanically via the I2 close walk and `bd mol ready --gated`.
- **Why one follow-up per `[h]` line, not rolled up**: easier triage
  in `bd human list`; each verifiable item is its own tracked unit;
  can close incrementally as humans verify.
- **Single-writer assumption for label updates**: brainstorm is
  interactive and single-session, so concurrent `bd label remove` /
  `bd label add` for `review-level:*` is unrealistic. Race documented
  but not defended against.
- **Mistakenly closed follow-up risk** (defensible to defer): if a
  user `bd close`s a human follow-up by mistake, the source bead's
  I2 walk closes the source and merge proceeds without the human
  check. The follow-up's description carries an explicit warning per
  R3, but the system does not enforce verification beyond that.
  Reopening the follow-up after merge does not un-merge the PR.
  Acceptable risk given the warning and the interactive nature of
  bd close.
- **Why the gate-step mechanism not a polling loop**: `bd mol ready
  --gated` is an existing primitive that surfaces blocked molecules
  for agent resume when their gate clears. No polling, no errored
  steps, no wasted molecule re-pours. The merge-and-cleanup gate
  step registers the source bead as its blocker; when the source
  closes (all children verified), the molecule appears in
  `bd mol ready --gated` and an agent picks it up.

## Dependencies

- **Hard dep on 7bk.3** — `bd dep add 7bk.12 7bk.3 --type blocks`.
  7bk.3 creates the `bead-spec` agent and adds its dispatch
  directives to the existing brainstorm-bead steps. Landing 7bk.3
  first means 7bk.12 inserts the new `classify-and-policy` step with
  the agent directive baked in (one pass through the file, no
  retrofit). The dep is a velocity / cleanliness call, not a
  correctness blocker — 7bk.12 could land first if 7bk.3 stalls,
  with the new step running in main session until 7bk.3 catches up.

## Open Questions

(None — `:` in label names verified, storage model verified, gate
mechanism verified.)

title: Production shell driver for autonomous bead processing (replaces run-queue skill)
Per-step claude -p orchestration: redesign bead pipeline (replace run-queue skill, rewrite implement-bead, define 7-stage feature workflow)
description:
Architectural redesign of the bead implementation pipeline. Replaces the run-queue skill and rewrites implement-bead to use a per-stage claude -p orchestration model. Defines a 7-stage feature workflow (with per-stage DoD, state-in/state-out, and configurable knobs) that bounds context growth, leverages bead/molecule structure as the persistence substrate, and respects Claude Code's "subagents cannot spawn subagents" rule.

## Summary

Today, run-queue runs as a skill in a single Claude session, repeatedly dispatching implement-bead which itself dispatches subagents per molecule step. Two structural problems: (1) the top-level session's context grows unboundedly across many beads; (2) implement-bead assumes step-wrapping subagents can re-dispatch leaf-work subagents, but per official Claude Code docs, subagents cannot spawn subagents.

This redesign:
1. Replaces run-queue with an external SHELL DRIVER that polls for implementation-ready beads and spawns one `claude -p` process per stage of the workflow.
2. Each stage's `claude -p` is the orchestrator for that stage; it can dispatch one level of subagents AND fire slash commands.
3. State that crosses stage boundaries lives in beads/labels/notes/filesystem — never in LLM context.
4. Defines a canonical 7-stage feature workflow with explicit DoD per stage. Stages are referenced by role name in implementation (`preflight`, `red-tests`, `green-loop`, `quality-sweep`, `verify-ac`, `create-pr`, `review-cycle`, `merge-or-handoff`); the "Stage N" numbering is design-discussion vocabulary only (see "Stage role names" table below).

## Background

Two prior beads (7bk.2 original scope, agents-config-1ck) and a discovery during the 7bk.2 brainstorm (session 5385d9fc-40dd-499c-8e57-959cf1192160) led here. Findings:
- *-developer agents deleted (commit 92fdfda; bead 1ck closed).
- code-debugger.md deleted in same commit.
- code-reviewer merged into quality-reviewer (commit 827ef3e).
- bead-verifier delivered in Phase 1 (7bk.1).
- /simplify is a slash COMMAND (not a skill); the code-simplifier agent is broken (phantom skill reference) — deletion tracked in 7bk.7.
- Subagents cannot fire slash commands; subagents cannot spawn subagents — per Claude Code docs.
- Run-queue's polling-loop-in-skill model causes unbounded context growth.

The original 7bk.2 (bead-implementor agent + worktree-label augment + formula text-only swaps) is superseded by this larger redesign. Discussion archived in molecule digest `agents-config-5c7`.

## The big architectural shift

Three cooperating layers:

1. **External shell driver** (NEW; replaces `run-queue` skill): polls bd for ready stage-beads in active molecules, spawns one `claude -p` process per ready stage, respects molecule DAG for sequencing.
2. **Per-stage claude -p invocation** (NEW orchestration model): each invocation drives ONE stage and exits. Inside, the orchestrator can dispatch one level of subagents and fire slash commands. Stable session-id (UUIDv5 from bead-id + stage-name) enables resumption.
3. **bead/molecule + filesystem persistence**: ALL state that must cross stage boundaries lives here. Worktree path → molecule label `worktree-path-*`. PR URL → label `pr-url-*`. Reviewer feedback → step-bead notes. Bug findings → new beads.

## The 7-stage feature workflow

(Stages are presented in execution order: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff. The numbered headings reflect the design's evolution; implementation MUST use the role names per the "Stage role names" section.)

### Stage 1: Spec validation + workflow classification
Fresh-eyes adversarial "is this enough detail to go on?" check on the implementation-ready bead. Defense in depth — does NOT redo brainstorm.

- Selects the appropriate formula by reading the bead's `formula-<name>` label (written during brainstorm-bead per Decision A). If no `formula-<name>` label is present, falls back to the canonical formula per bead type:
  - `feature` → `implement-feature`
  - `bug` → `fix-bug`
  - `task` → `implement-feature` (treated as feature-shaped)
  - `chore` → `implement-feature` (no separate chore formula in scope; revisit if patterns diverge)
  - `epic` → flag-human (epics decompose into children; there is no formula for an epic itself)
  - Unknown formula label → flag-human.
- Brainstorm-bead label-overwrite contract: when brainstorm-bead is re-run on a bead, it MUST first remove any existing `formula-<name>`, `auto-mergeable-true`, `iteration-cap-*-<n>`, `review-exit-*`, and `coverage-threshold-<n>` labels before stamping new ones, so stale knobs cannot survive a re-brainstorm.
- Verifies project quality-gate config; marks the quality-sweep stage skippable if no checks beyond RALF-IT's defaults.
- Verifies a standard coverage report location exists for the project (per-module + project). If absent → flag-human (inadequate tooling).
- Worktree-vs-pour failure ordering: pour the formula FIRST, then create the worktree. If pour fails, no worktree is created and there is nothing to clean up. If worktree creation fails after pour, `bd mol squash <mol-id>` the molecule with the failure summary and flag-human; do NOT leave a poured molecule without a worktree.
- Stamps `worktree-path-*` label on the molecule once both succeed (encoding spec is in "Worktree-path label encoding" below).
- DoD: poured molecule + worktree exists + `worktree-path-*` label stamped, OR `human` label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

### Stage 2: TDD red phase (write failing tests)
- Skill: superpowers:test-driven-development + superpowers:writing-unit-tests + superpowers:testing-anti-patterns.
- Two-reviewer protocol:
  - Claude: invoke the `test-review` skill.
  - Codex: adversarial review of added tests in context of surrounding tests (default model `gpt-5.5`); flag redundant/contradictory.
- Test-review checklist:
  - Cover the AC.
  - No test-only methods leaking into production.
  - No mocks of unverified dependencies.
  - Edge / error paths.
  - Minimal white-box: verify functionality, not implementation detail.
  - No tests of 3rd-party behaviors.
  - Single-responsibility unit tests (no long when-then-when-then chains).
  - Combo of unit + integration tests covering anticipated architecture and functional outcomes.
  - Reviewer asks: "Do these tests reflect reasonable design?"
- Iteration cap n=2. At n+1, write disagreement note to state-out. Issues may resolve in Stage 3 or surface in PR comments. Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.
- Reviewer disagreement, "more conservative" tiebreak rule: when one reviewer flags a finding as critical/major and the other does not, treat as if the more-severe rating wins UNLESS the silent reviewer explicitly approved that aspect. If neither silently-approved nor explicitly-flagged, the finding stands at the higher severity. The orchestrator records this resolution in state-out so the implementor can address it in Stage 3.
- Modified tests get the same rules as new tests.
- DoD: tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state.

### Stage 3: Implementation (green phase) via RALF-IT
**Stage 3 = invoke the RALF-IT skill with MAX_ITERATIONS=5.**

RALF-IT handles:
- Implementation subagent dispatch.
- Quality gate (build/typecheck/lint/test) between every iteration.
- Foreign-eyes review: Codex (iter 1), Gemini (iter 2), pure Claude fresh-eyes (iter 3+).
- Foreign-agent failure → degrade to pure fresh-eyes (iteration still counts).
- Anti-bias rules (don't tell eyes-subagent which iteration it is; give original spec not prior summary).
- Final review pass.
- Foreign-CLI invocations: `codex-companion.mjs task --model gpt-5.4` and `gemini -p "" --approval-mode plan -o text`.

Coverage:
- Threshold from project-config.toml `[coverage].threshold`, default 80% if no project/AC override.
- Coverage threshold is an INDICATOR, not a quality measure. Implementor + reviewers focus on quality of tests, not metric satisfaction.
- Coverage gap awareness: watch for new branches introduced by implementation choices not covered by Stage 2's tests.

Unverifiable/unimplementable triggers (flag-human + abort):
- AC bullet requires resources the chunk can't access (third-party API, secret, hardware).
- AC bullet contradicts an earlier AC bullet (caught at Stage 1, but might surface here).
- Implementation requires architectural changes outside the bead's stated scope.
- Tests pass in isolation but not in integration (root cause outside bead's scope).
- No standard coverage report location for the project (also caught at Stage 1).

Acknowledge Stage 2 deferred issues explicitly in state-out (invalidated / resolved / surfaced in PR).

DoD: RALF-IT reports complete; tests green; coverage ≥ target; build/typecheck/lint pass.

### Stage 5: Global quality sweep
**Optional** — Stage 1 marks skippable if project-config.toml introduces no checks beyond RALF-IT's gates.

- Scope: GLOBAL (entire project, not just RALF-IT's changed files).
- Includes additional static-analysis tools from project-config.toml `[static-analysis]` (semgrep, depcheck, etc.).
- Order: lint auto-fixes FIRST (`[lint-autofix].command`), then build/typecheck/lint, then static-analysis, then tests. ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes — guarantees Stage 6 starts green.
- Auto-fix vs flag-human: auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw (require fundamental design changes). Implementor uses judgment based on size of fix.

DoD: all gate commands exit zero.

### Stage 6: Final validation against AC
Mechanical verification of each AC bullet against worktree state.

- AC bullets pre-classified by brainstorm-bead into `## Acceptance Criteria (Mechanical)` and `## Acceptance Criteria (Human Review)` sections (see 7bk.12).
- Stage 6 ONLY validates mechanical bullets. Human-review bullets become separate post-implementation beads marked for human-only processing (do NOT hold up molecule).
- Run additional functional/UI tests from project-config.toml `[functional-tests]` (e.g. e2e, UI tests).
- Validation report: persisted to BOTH bead notes AND PR description.
- If functional tests fail at this stage: create one bug bead per failure, flag each human-needed, note in PR comments. Do NOT loop back, do NOT auto-revert. Molecule continues.

DoD: every mechanical AC bullet has explicit witness evidence; human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed.

### Stage 7a: Create PR
Fast, deterministic.
- Skill: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.
- PR description includes: bead reference + AC, RALF-IT iteration summary, Stage 6 AC validation report, bug beads filed during Stage 6, human-required AC follow-up bead references.
- PR creation failure → flag-human; clean exit; leave worktree intact.
- Persists `pr-url-*` label on the bead.

DoD: PR exists, URL persisted on bead.

### Stage 7b: Review cycle
Long-running polling.
- Skill: superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads).
- One iteration = one outbound reply-batch: poll PR until new review activity or timeout, address all open FIX-class threads in one commit + reply pass, push, mark threads resolved on GitHub. The cap counts batches, not polls.
- Iteration cap: 5 batches with early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.
- Exit conditions configurable per bead (Copilot only / Copilot + N human approvers / etc.) — knob set during brainstorm.
- Stable session-id (UUIDv5 from bead-id + role name `review-cycle`) enables checkpoint+resume across crashes or driver restarts.

DoD: every review thread has a reply; FIXED items resolved on GitHub; no in-flight FIX work.

### Stage 8: Merge or hand-off
Branches based on the bead's `auto-mergeable-true` knob label (set during brainstorm; default OFF — i.e. label absent means hand-off).

- **Auto-merge path** (`auto-mergeable-true` label present): pour `merge-and-cleanup` formula, drive it. Cleanup = branch deletion (local + remote), worktree removal (decoded from label), parent-chain close-walk (I2 invariant).
- **Hand-off path** (default, label absent): transition the original bead to bd status `open` and stamp two labels: `merge-ready` (the discriminator that tells the human *why* the bead is on their queue) and `human` (the existing human-flag protocol marker that surfaces it via `bd human list`). The bead does NOT close until the human runs `/merge-and-cleanup`, which (a) merges the PR, (b) executes the cleanup actions of merge-and-cleanup formula, and (c) closes the bead with a parent-chain close-walk (I2 invariant). Naming follows the `implementation-ready` convention.

  **I1 exception, deliberately documented (Decision C / C1c)**: the original bead transitions from `in_progress` back to `open` for the hand-off. This appears to violate I1's "claim-walk = mark in_progress when work begins" invariant. It is deliberate: by Stage 8's hand-off path, all automated work is complete; the bead is blocked on a human action (merging the PR). Returning to `open` keeps the bead visible to the standard `bd ready` query semantics — combined with the `human` and `merge-ready` labels, the bead surfaces in `bd human list` only, not in `bd ready`. Rationale: keep bead-query semantics consistent across the codebase rather than invent a new status. The `human` label gates `bd ready` exclusion; the `merge-ready` label tells the human *why* the bead is on their queue. Re-claim by an automated agent is prevented by the `human` label, not by status.

DoD: appropriate path executed.

## Cross-cutting infrastructure

### Project config: `./project-config.toml` at PROJECT ROOT
Sections:
- `[gates]` — default quality-gate commands.
- `[coverage]` — threshold (default 80), report-location, per-module-pattern, format.
- `[lint-autofix]` — auto-fix command for Stage 5.
- `[static-analysis]` — extra checks for Stage 5; if absent → Stage 5 skippable.
- `[functional-tests]` — UI/e2e commands for Stage 6.
- `[review-requirements]` — copilot-required, human-approvers-required defaults.

NO "chunk" / "stage" naming in section headers — discussion vocabulary, not design surface.

### Severity vocabulary (uniform across all reviewer stages)
- **critical**: ship-stopper / safety / data loss.
- **major**: correctness or design flaw warranting escalation.
- **minor**: quality issue, not ship-stopping.
- **nit**: preference, drop.

### Stage role names (numbering vs label vocabulary)
The "Stage 1, 2, 3, ..." vocabulary is design discussion only. It reflects the sequence and history of the workflow's evolution. Numbering MUST NOT bleed into labels, formula step IDs, slash-command names, code identifiers, or any other implementation surface. Implementation uses role names:

| Stage # (discussion only) | Role name (used in labels, code, file names) |
|---|---|
| Stage 1 | `preflight` |
| Stage 2 | `red-tests` |
| Stage 3 | `green-loop` |
| Stage 5 | `quality-sweep` |
| Stage 6 | `verify-ac` |
| Stage 7a | `create-pr` |
| Stage 7b | `review-cycle` |
| Stage 8 | `merge-or-handoff` |

This decouples implementation from the design's numbering and keeps the spec resilient to future re-sequencing.

### Iteration limits
Defaults hard-coded per stage (`red-tests`: n=2; `green-loop` RALF-IT: n=5; `review-cycle`: n=5). Per-bead override via brainstorm-time knob labels (see below). Each label is the SOLE override path for that limit; RALF-IT does not honor any other override mechanism for MAX_ITERATIONS.

### Brainstorm-time knob label vocabulary
Per Decision B, the label vocabulary is defined in THIS bead. brainstorm-bead writes labels onto the bead at finalization; the preflight stage (and downstream stages) read them with default fallbacks. 7bk.12 owns the brainstorm-UX of asking the user the knob questions and writing these labels — it does NOT define new vocabulary.

Reader contract (universal): each label is independent and idempotent. If absent, the stage uses its default. If present with a malformed value, flag-human. If multiple mutually-exclusive labels appear (e.g. two `formula-*`), flag-human.

Defined labels (role-named, not numbered):

| Label | Read by | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `formula-<name>` | preflight | per bead type (feature → `implement-feature`, bug → `fix-bug`, task → `implement-feature`, chore → `implement-feature`, epic → flag-human) | Names the formula variant to pour. Future variants (e.g. `implement-feature-simple`) plug in here without code changes. |
| `auto-mergeable-true` | merge-or-handoff | absent (hand-off path) | When present, the merge-or-handoff stage takes the auto-merge branch instead of the human hand-off branch. |
| `iteration-cap-red-tests-<n>` | red-tests | 2 | Override red-tests review-loop cap. `<n>` is a positive integer. |
| `iteration-cap-green-loop-<n>` | green-loop (RALF-IT) | 5 | Override RALF-IT MAX_ITERATIONS. `<n>` is a positive integer. SOLE override path. |
| `iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n>` | review-cycle | 5 | Override review-cycle iteration cap (one iteration = one outbound reply-batch). `<n>` is a positive integer. |
| `review-exit-copilot-only` | review-cycle | this default | Exit when Copilot review completes; ignore human-approver count. |
| `review-exit-human-approvers-<n>` | review-cycle | absent | Require `<n>` human approvers (in addition to Copilot) before review-cycle exits. Mutually exclusive with `review-exit-copilot-only`. |
| `coverage-threshold-<n>` | green-loop (RALF-IT), quality-sweep | from `[coverage].threshold` in project-config.toml; if absent there, 80 | Override the project's default coverage threshold for THIS bead. `<n>` is an integer percent 0-100. |

Until 7bk.12 lands, these labels can be applied manually via `bd label add <bead-id> <label>` if a bead needs a non-default value. The preflight stage must work whether or not 7bk.12 is in place.

### bd label charset
Observed bd labels in the existing codebase use lowercase letters (a-z), digits (0-9), hyphen (`-`), underscore (`_`), and dot (`.`). All labels defined by this spec stay within that alphabet. Implementations MUST validate that all labels they generate (especially `worktree-path-*` encoded values) stay within `[a-z0-9._-]` plus `__` (the path separator escape) and `_u` (the underscore escape). Characters outside this set MUST be rejected at encode time with a flag-human note.

### Human-flag protocol
- Stage sets `human` label on step-bead, transitions step-bead status to `open` (NOT in_progress), appends structured note, exits cleanly.
- Shell driver / `bd ready` filter excludes beads with `human` label.
- Human removes `human` label → bead reappears in ready list naturally on next poll.
- Re-pickup is fresh: chunk reads inputs, decides idempotently. Human's note becomes part of state-in.

### Session-id convention
- claude -p `--session-id` requires a valid UUID.
- Use UUIDv5 with the pinned namespace UUID below + input string `<bead-id>:<stage-role-name>` so re-invocation auto-resumes.
- **Pinned namespace UUID** (do NOT change once shipped — changing it invalidates all existing resumable sessions): `27ece4fd-4a06-49bf-a921-bf07ecb0dc10`
- Stage-role-name is from the "Stage role names" table above (`preflight`, `red-tests`, `green-loop`, `quality-sweep`, `verify-ac`, `create-pr`, `review-cycle`, `merge-or-handoff`).
- `--name <name>` is for display only (resume picker, terminal title) — not used as session ID.

### Per-stage claude -p invocation contract
- Default invocation: `claude -p --session-id <uuidv5> "/implement-bead <bead-id>"`.
- Default system prompt; bead is self-explanatory. Additional `--append-system-prompt` only if needed.
- **cwd contract per stage** (the shell driver sets cwd before spawning `claude -p`):
  - `preflight`: cwd = repo root (the worktree does not yet exist; preflight creates it).
  - `red-tests`, `green-loop`, `quality-sweep`, `verify-ac`, `create-pr`, `review-cycle`: cwd = the worktree path decoded from the molecule's `worktree-path-*` label. All commits, builds, and tests run inside the worktree.
  - `merge-or-handoff`: cwd = repo root (the worktree may be torn down by this stage; the merge runs from the main checkout).
- **Slash-command discovery from worktrees**: `/implement-bead` is a project-scoped slash command at `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md`. Claude Code resolves project-scoped commands by walking up from cwd to find a `.claude/` directory. Worktrees are full git checkouts at `.claude/worktrees/<name>/`; they have their own copy of `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md` (because the file is checked in). The smoke-test (Implementation scope #11) MUST verify slash-command discovery from a worktree subdirectory and document the resolution path.
- Slash-command invocation requires slash-commands enabled (default; broken only by `--disable-slash-commands`).

### Worktree-path label encoding
- Format: `worktree-path-<encoded-path>`.
- Reversible (bijective) encoding using a double-character escape so the decode is unambiguous regardless of literal underscores or `__` substrings in the path:
  - **Encode** (apply IN ORDER):
    1. `_` → `_u`
    2. `/` → `__`
  - **Decode** (apply IN ORDER, the reverse):
    1. `__` → `/`
    2. `_u` → `_`
- Trailing slash on the input path is stripped before encoding.
- Validation: the encoded value MUST match `[a-z0-9._-]+` (with `_` permitted). If the input path contains characters outside `[A-Za-z0-9._/_-]`, encode-time validation fails and the orchestrator flags-human (do NOT silently corrupt the label).
- Trace example: input `a__b/c` → step 1 → `a_u_ub/c` → step 2 → `a_u_ub__c`. Decode `a_u_ub__c` → step 1 → `a_u_ub/c` → step 2 → `a__b/c`. ✓ round-trips.
- Implementations MUST include unit tests for round-trip across: paths with literal `_`, paths with literal `__`, paths with multiple `/`, trailing-slash inputs, and at least one realistic worktree path (e.g. `.claude/worktrees/agents-config-7bk.9-impl`).

### Stage idempotent re-entry (resume semantics)
A `claude -p` process is not infinitely-lived; the shell driver may restart it, the host may crash, or the green-loop stage may take long enough that the user kills and re-runs it. Every stage's claude -p MUST be idempotent on re-entry:

- **preflight, create-pr, merge-or-handoff**: structurally idempotent. Re-running re-checks state (worktree exists? PR exists? merge done?) and skips work already complete.
- **red-tests, quality-sweep, verify-ac**: idempotent because their work products live in the filesystem (committed tests, lint fixes, AC validation report appended to bead notes). Re-running re-evaluates state and continues from where it left off.
- **green-loop (RALF-IT)**: the iteration counter is persisted to the green-loop step-bead's notes after each iteration completes, in a single-line marker `RALF-IT-ITER: <n>/<MAX>`. On re-entry, RALF-IT reads the marker (default 0 if absent) and resumes at iteration `<n>+1`. The iteration cap counts toward the global cap regardless of how many `claude -p` processes participated.
- **review-cycle**: the batch counter is persisted to the review-cycle step-bead's notes after each batch (`REVIEW-BATCH: <n>/<MAX>`). PR thread state is the source of truth — the counter is just for cap enforcement.
- All stages: filesystem work (commits, push) is idempotent because git's content-addressed model deduplicates. Re-running an already-pushed commit is a no-op.

### State channels (per transition)
| Transition | Channel |
|---|---|
| Bead → preflight | bead description, AC sections |
| preflight → red-tests | poured molecule, `worktree-path-*` label, bead notes |
| red-tests → green-loop | filesystem (worktree, feature branch, committed tests), step-bead notes |
| green-loop → quality-sweep | RALF-IT final report (in step-bead notes), filesystem |
| quality-sweep → verify-ac | filesystem (green build), step-bead notes |
| verify-ac → create-pr | bead notes (validation report), filesystem, new bug bead refs |
| create-pr → review-cycle | `pr-url-*` label, bead notes |
| review-cycle → merge-or-handoff | bead notes (review-cycle complete), `pr-url-*` label |

## Implementation scope (this bead)

1. **Rewrite `implement-bead` skill** — its job becomes "execute ONE stage of a molecule and exit," not "drive the entire molecule." Per-stage orchestrator behavior. Reads the appropriate stage step-bead, dispatches subagents/skills/slash commands per the stage's spec, persists state-out to beads/filesystem, exits. Stays a SKILL (not converted to slash command).

2. **Add `/implement-bead` slash command wrapper** (per Decision E) — thin wrapper at `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md` that takes `$ARGUMENTS` (the bead-id) and invokes the implement-bead skill. Both invocation paths supported: (a) interactive Claude sessions reach the skill via discovery, (b) the shell driver's `claude -p` reaches it deterministically via the slash command. The skill remains the source of truth for methodology; the command is a six-line dispatcher.

3. **Rewrite `start-bead` skill** — Route A's hand-off destination changes. Instead of "run-queue picks it up in a dedicated session," it becomes "the shell driver picks it up." Routing logic otherwise preserved.

The naming convention and selection mechanism is fixed by Decision A: brainstorm-bead writes a `formula-<name>` label; Stage 1 reads it; default fallback per bead type. Variants (e.g. `implement-feature-simple`, `fix-bug-hotfix`) plug in by adding a `<name>.formula.toml` and updating the formulary index — no Stage 1 code changes required.

Includes:

The AC is "uses the current canonical reviewer-agent name from `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`," not a hard-coded `quality-reviewer` string.

11. **End-to-end smoke test against scratch fixture project** (per Decision D, refined per M6) — `scripts/smoke/setup.sh` creates a fresh, isolated scratch project in a `/tmp/` directory (e.g. `/tmp/bead-pipeline-smoke-<timestamp>/`) and initializes it with `bd init`. The scratch project gets:
    - `package.json` (or equivalent) with a real test runner (recommended: TypeScript + vitest, since other agents-config skills already lean TS).
    - At least one trivial source file and one trivial passing test.

    The scratch project is NOT under version control of the agents-config repo; it lives in `/tmp/` exclusively to keep the agents-config bd database free of smoke-test pollution. `scripts/bead-driver-test.sh` is invoked with the scratch project as its working root (it inherits the scratch project's `.beads/*.db` automatically because bd auto-discovers from cwd).

    Smoke acceptance: when `scripts/bead-driver-test.sh` is run inside the scratch project, all stages (preflight, red-tests, green-loop, quality-sweep, verify-ac, create-pr, review-cycle, merge-or-handoff) execute and exit cleanly, the molecule completes, and the scratch project's PR (or hand-off bead) materializes. title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation. Prefer epic-sibling
   placement over orphan-with-dep:

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')

     # Decide placement via the sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3):
     # would this work have been on the parent epic's original plan, if
     # we'd thought of it? Yes → sibling. No → orphan + discovered-from.
     IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=false  # set to true only when the answer is yes

     if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && [ "$IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK" = true ]; then
       # Create INSIDE the parent epic as a sibling:
       bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --parent "$PARENT"
     else
       # Otherwise create as an orphan and link with discovered-from:
       NEW=$(bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --json | jq -r '.id')
       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   Sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3): would this discovered work
   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>

formula, the first `bd update` is a no-op — re-running it is safe.

title: Delete code-simplifier agent; rewire completion-gate to invoke /simplify directly
notes:
## Update (agents-config-7bk.8 brainstorm session, 2026-05-02)

**Architecture flip — this bead's scope has narrowed.**

Original Path C ("Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator") is OBSOLETE. New plan:
- agents-config-b7s creates a portable `simplify` skill (ported from the /simplify command body)
- agents-config-7bk.8 rewrites `src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` step 3 (and ~10 other dispatch sites across the codebase) to invoke the new `simplify` skill — uniform invocation, no orchestrator-tier-only guard
- This bead (7bk.7) now ONLY deletes the broken agent file at `src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md`

**New dependency**: agents-config-7bk.7 now depends on agents-config-7bk.8 (the sweep must complete before the agent file deletion, otherwise stale dispatches pointing at a non-existent agent would proliferate).

**Original notes (preserved for context, but now partially obsolete):**

---

## Discovery (verbatim from session 5385d9fc)

1. /simplify is a slash command, NOT a skill. It lives outside this repo (installed via a plugin or sidekick injection — not present in src/). When invoked, its body is loaded into the session as a prompt.
2. The /simplify command is itself a parallel-subagent dispatcher. Its Phase 2 explicitly says: "Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message" — three reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency), then aggregate, then apply fixes.
3. The code-simplifier agent at src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md is BROKEN. It instructs itself to "invoke the bundled /simplify skill … via the Skill tool with skill name simplify" — but no such skill exists. It points at a phantom dependency.
4. Subagents CANNOT invoke slash commands (those are user/orchestrator-initiated), so a simple "fix the reference" patch will NOT work.
5. completion-gate.md step 3 currently calls the code-simplifier agent.

## Decision (Path C) — SUPERSEDED, see Update above

Delete the code-simplifier agent layer entirely. Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator (the orchestrator CAN run slash commands).

## What must be preserved (currently encoded in the agent — needs a new home)

- Worktree scoping (operate in the supplied worktree path, not the main tree)
- Behavior-preserving discipline (apply-vs-flag classification, "no abstractions without ≥ 2 call sites")
- Project memory persistence (.claude/agent-memory/code-simplifier/ recurring-pattern capture)
- Boundary rules (no scope creep, no new dependencies, no test rewrites, no commits/pushes)

→ NEW HOME: agents-config-b7s ports these into the `simplify` skill body.

## Files involved (revised)

- src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md — DELETE (this bead)
- src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md — handled by 7bk.8, not this bead
- /simplify command source — handled by b7s
- Any other rules/skills referencing code-simplifier agent — handled by 7bk.8 sweep

## Suggested execution steps (revised)

1. Verify 7bk.8 sweep is complete (`bd show 7bk.8` status=closed).
2. Confirm `grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/` returns empty (the sweep should have made it so).
3. Delete src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md.
4. Run completion gate on the change.

## Relationship to agents-config-7bk.2

Phase 2 (7bk.2) of the agent fleet epic is in flight. Original coordination still applies — 7bk.2's formula updates land independently.

title: Phase 2: bead-implementor agent + formula rewiring
description:
Phase 2 of agents-config-7bk. Wires the new specialized agent fleet into the bead pipeline by introducing the bead-implementor agent, augmenting implement-bead with worktree lifetime ownership, and rewiring the implement-feature and fix-bug formulas to dispatch the right agents for review/implement/verify and to invoke /simplify directly (orchestrator-side) for the simplify gate.

## Summary

Add a single named "implementor" agent (bead-implementor) that subagents are dispatched as for write-tests, implement, and diagnose steps. Move worktree creation/teardown from per-step responsibility into the implement-bead orchestrator, persisting the worktree path on the molecule via a label so every dispatched subagent receives it. Update both implementation formulas to use current agent names (post Phase 1 + post *-developer cleanup), and convert the simplify step from subagent dispatch to orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation.

## Background

Originally specced 2026-04-26 alongside the dynamic-dove plan. The world has shifted since:
- *-developer agents were deleted (commit 92fdfda; bead 1ck closed); the dynamic-dove "preload domain skills by subject area" justification is gone.
- code-debugger.md was deleted in the same commit; superpowers:systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing replace it.
- code-reviewer was merged into quality-reviewer (commit 827ef3e).
- bead-verifier was delivered in Phase 1 (7bk.1) but is not yet wired into any formula.
- Formulas still reference stale agent paths (superpowers:code-reviewer, code-simplifier:code-simplifier).
- DISCOVERY (session 5385d9fc): /simplify is a slash COMMAND (not a skill), and the code-simplifier agent is broken (points at a phantom skill). Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so the simplify step must be orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched. The actual deletion of the code-simplifier agent is tracked separately in agents-config-7bk.7 (peer cleanup bead, non-blocking).

This bead consolidates the surviving Phase 2 work: add bead-implementor as the implementing/debugging muscle, wire bead-verifier into the verify gate, fix stale agent references, centralize worktree lifecycle in the orchestrator, and convert the simplify step to orchestrator-direct invocation.

## Requirements

1. **CREATE `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`** — minimal "implementor" agent.
   - Frontmatter: name=bead-implementor, model=sonnet, color=blue, tools=Read+Edit+Write+Grep+Glob+Bash, memory=project.
   - skills: [superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns, superpowers:using-git-worktrees, superpowers:verification-before-completion, superpowers:systematic-debugging, superpowers:root-cause-tracing]
   - Body: cd into the worktree path passed by the orchestrator (via prompt — derived from molecule label, see #2), follow preloaded TDD methodology for write-tests/implement steps, follow preloaded debugging skills for diagnose steps. Never declare done without verification evidence. NO domain-skill preloading machinery — just the fixed skills list above.

2. **AUGMENT `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md`** — orchestrator owns the worktree for the bead's full lifetime.
   - On the FIRST dispatched step that needs a worktree (typically write-tests for features, diagnose for bugs), the orchestrator invokes superpowers:using-git-worktrees, captures the path, and stamps a label on the molecule of the form `worktree-path-<sanitized>` where slashes in the path are replaced by `__` (decode on read).
   - On every subsequent subagent dispatch, the orchestrator parses the label off the molecule and injects the decoded worktree path into the subagent's prompt.
   - Worktree teardown happens in the merge-and-cleanup formula (out of scope for this bead beyond noting the contract — merge-and-cleanup already exists).
   - Document the label format and parse/encode helpers in the SKILL.md so future agents can interpret the convention.

3. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`:**
   - Replace `Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer` → `Agent: quality-reviewer` (review step).
   - Convert simplify step from `Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier` to ORCHESTRATOR-DIRECT: the step body instructs the orchestrator to invoke the `/simplify` slash command directly (no Agent: line), since /simplify itself dispatches three parallel subagents internally. Document this in the step text so future readers understand why simplify is structured differently from review/verify.
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into write-tests and implement steps (replacing whatever generic dispatch is currently there).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

4. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:**
   - Same review and simplify changes as #3 (review = quality-reviewer; simplify = orchestrator-direct /simplify).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into the diagnose, write-tests, and implement steps (bead-implementor's preloaded systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing skills cover diagnose).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

5. **DROP from original spec:** no longer modify code-debugger.md (file deleted in 92fdfda; debugging skills now live as preloaded skills on bead-implementor per #1).

6. **End-to-end smoke test:** push a trivial bead through run-queue and confirm the new dispatches fire correctly — bead-implementor runs in the persisted worktree, bead-verifier reports exit codes, quality-reviewer runs in the review step, and the simplify step's orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation completes (or is skipped cleanly if 7bk.7 hasn't landed yet — confirm graceful behavior either way). Worktree label stamps on the molecule and parses correctly across step boundaries.

## Out of Scope

- Domain-skill preloading on bead-implementor (1ck closed; *-developer skills not built).
- Deletion of src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md and rewriting of completion-gate.md step 3 — tracked in agents-config-7bk.7.
- Editing the /simplify command body (also 7bk.7 territory if that bead chooses to add a Phase 0 discipline section).
- Teardown logic in merge-and-cleanup beyond confirming the existing flow can read the label-stored path.
- Changes to brainstorm-bead.formula.toml (no agent dispatch in brainstorming).
- Updating tech-lead.md or any other surviving agent (Phase 1 territory or already done).
- Replacing the existing `for-bead-<bead-id>` molecule label convention (orthogonal; worktree-path label is additive).

## Design Notes

- **Why a single bead-implementor instead of multiple specialized "implementor" variants:** the original dynamic-dove rationale was domain-skill preloading. With *-developer skills not built, there's no useful axis on which to specialize. One named role keeps formula entries traceable and gives a clear seam if domain preloading is ever revived.
- **Why label storage for worktree path:** labels are lifecycle-neutral, queryable via existing bd CLI, and don't entangle the molecule's notes/description (which formulas may overwrite). Sanitization is a known cost; the alternative (mol notes) gets clobbered by step logic that uses --notes.
- **Why bead-implementor handles diagnose:** option (a) from the brainstorm Q3 — preloading systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing on a single agent is cheaper than building a parallel bead-debugger and gives the formula one consistent dispatch target across feature/bug pipelines.
- **Why simplify is orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched:** /simplify is a slash command (not a skill) and itself dispatches three parallel reviewer subagents internally. Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so wrapping /simplify in an "Agent: code-simplifier" dispatch is structurally impossible. The orchestrator (the agent driving the molecule) IS the only layer that can run /simplify, so the formula step body instructs it directly.
- **Worktree label encoding:** `worktree-path-<path-with-slashes-replaced-by-double-underscore>`. Decode by reversing the replacement. Example: `/Users/scott/foo/bar` → `worktree-path-__Users__scott__foo__bar`. Skills/agents that read this MUST parse from the well-known prefix.
- **Relationship with 7bk.7:** 7bk.7 (delete code-simplifier agent + rewrite completion-gate step 3) is a peer cleanup, not a blocker. 7bk.2's formula updates avoid referencing the doomed agent regardless of when 7bk.7 lands. They are independent.

## Acceptance Criteria

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with frontmatter and body matching requirement #1.
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md documents and implements the worktree-label lifecycle (create-on-first-need, stamp, parse-and-inject on subsequent dispatches).
- implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier:code-simplifier (simplify step is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify.
- A trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes successfully with the new dispatches firing in the expected steps.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Dependencies

- ✓ agents-config-7bk.1 (Phase 1 — bead-verifier, quality-reviewer delivered)
- ✓ agents-config-1ck (closed: *-developer agents removed; dynamic-dove preload superseded)
- (peer, non-blocking) agents-config-7bk.7 — cleanup of the code-simplifier agent layer

title: Externalize long bead specs to docs/beads/ files; bead description stays a short summary referencing the doc
notes:
Context: while brainstorming lu3.4, the final spec grew to ~300 lines. Writing the full spec into the bead notes field via 'bd update --notes' requires shell-escape gymnastics and replaces the field wholesale (see bug guard in lu3.4 spec R6). Manually archived lu3.4 spec to docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md to preserve it.

Proposal: adopt a pattern where long bead specs live at docs/beads/<bead-id>-<slug>.md (or docs/specs/<date>-<slug>.md), and the bead's description/notes is a short summary + reference to the doc file path. The bead remains self-descriptive; the full spec is versioned in git alongside code.

Benefits:
- shell-safe authoring (edit the file directly, no CLI string escaping)
- PR-reviewable spec changes via normal diff review
- grep-friendly
- no duplication between bd's notes field and docs/specs
- bead description stays scannable in 'bd show <id>'

Open questions (for start-bead/brainstorm-bead to resolve, not now):
- Threshold for 'long enough' to externalize (line count? number of sections?)
- Should brainstorm-bead.formula.toml's write-spec step be updated to write long specs to file + summary to bd notes?
- Should create-bead / start-bead skills know about this pattern?
- Directory choice: reuse docs/specs/ vs new docs/beads/ subdir?
- How does the bead reference the doc — relative path? URL to repo? Commit-pinned?
- Do we need a 'seal' mechanism to prevent bd notes from drifting out of sync with the external doc?

title: wait-for-pr-comments: poll-copilot-review.sh returns stale reviews on re-review cycles
description:
# Problem

After pushing fixes and re-requesting Copilot review, poll-copilot-review.sh (in the wait-for-pr-comments skill) short-circuits on attempt 1 and reports copilot_review_found — but returns the STALE prior review rather than the new one. The JSON it emits has a commit_id field pointing at the OLD commit, which the skill's Phase 3 triage then operates on.

# Impact

Auto-fix loop spins on the same comments it already addressed. Discovered on agents-config-uvt PR #12 during two re-review cycles; had to fall back to manual gh api polling with a submitted_at > last-commit-timestamp filter.

# Root cause

The script searches /pulls/N/reviews for ANY review authored by Copilot. It doesn't compare commit_id against the latest pushed commit, nor submitted_at against the re-request time.

# Fix direction

poll-copilot-review.sh should accept an optional --since-commit or --since-timestamp argument, and the Phase 4 re-review flow in wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md should pass the latest commit's timestamp so only newer reviews match.

# Discovered-from

agents-config-uvt

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
Second repro on scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick PR #183 (2026-05-02):

- 11:05Z: Copilot review #1 with 4 inline findings.
- After fixes pushed, copilot_work_started fired at 14:08:33Z.
- poll-copilot-review.sh --skip-request-check entered sub-phase C, immediately found review #1 (predates work_started), reported 'Copilot review found (attempt 1)' and exited 0.
- Skill emitted Variant 1 'All fixed, no Copilot re-review' final report.
- Actual second review landed at 14:14:38Z (6 minutes later) with 6 NEW inline findings — caught manually, not by the script.

Adds two UX dimensions worth fixing alongside the timestamp filter:

1. Risk multiplier: a false 'All clear' Variant 1 report is the exact moment a user authorizes merge ('merge it' / 'ship it'). The skill needs to express uncertainty when a re-review is in flight, not declare clear.

2. Pre-trigger hint: even before copilot_work_started fires, the existence of commits with author.date > last_review.submitted_at is a strong signal a re-review may be queued. The report should surface that timing relationship explicitly so users can wait if needed.

Originally filed as scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick:sidekick-aga3 (now deleted as duplicate of this bead).

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. If all siblings in a parent epic are now closed, close the parent:
     bd show {{bead-id}}    # check for parent
     bd list --parent=<parent-id>  # check siblings

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation:
     bd create "Found: ..." -t bug -p 1
     bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:
- Tests must be written against the acceptance criteria from the brainstorm step
- Cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand
- Do NOT write test-only methods in production code

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.

- Who uses it and how?
- What does "done" look like? (acceptance criteria)

- Any architectural implications or constraints?
- Is there a simpler approach that solves the same problem?

Output: written acceptance criteria and a clear implementation plan.

title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

- Logic that could be expressed more simply

description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:
- Tests must be written against the acceptance criteria from the brainstorm step
- Cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand
- Do NOT write test-only methods in production code

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.

title: Completion gate: code review
description:
Dispatch code-reviewer subagent against ALL changed files.

Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer
Scope: every file changed since branch creation, reviewed against:
  - The implementation plan from the brainstorm step
  - SOLID and DRY principles
  - Security by design (OWASP top 10, input validation, no injection paths)
  - Architectural consistency with the rest of the codebase
  - The standards in INSTRUCTIONS.md

Address ALL findings before proceeding. If a finding is deferred,

For high-stakes changes (security-sensitive, architecture shifts):

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. If all siblings in a parent epic are now closed, close the parent:
     bd show {{bead-id}}    # check for parent
     bd list --parent=<parent-id>  # check siblings

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation:
     bd create "Found: ..." -t bug -p 1
     bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>

notes:
# Documentation pass for public repo readiness

## Summary
Prepare the agents-config repo for public consumption. Scope: polish existing top-level docs, correct known drift, and fill gaps a first-time visitor expects (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, per-subfolder READMEs). No GitHub-specific scaffolding, no prose-level rewrites.

## Background / motivation
Repo is going public. Current state: root README.md is detailed but has drift from the rules/ reorganization; root AGENTS.md has duplicated session-completion blocks; tool-specific subfolders under src/user/ have no or near-empty READMEs; no LICENSE file exists despite README declaring MIT; no CONTRIBUTING.md. A stranger landing on the repo needs accurate install guidance and a clear picture of what each folder contains.

Primary reader: fork-and-install users, not contributors. Light contributor notes only.

## Requirements
- Add a root LICENSE file matching the MIT license already declared in README.md. Copyright holder: "Scott Hamilton". Year: 2026.
- Add a short CONTRIBUTING.md acknowledging the repo is a personal config and asking contributors to open an issue first.
- Add a README.md to each tool-specific subfolder: src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, src/user/.codex/, src/user/.gemini/. Each explains purpose, contents, install target, and intended audience.
- Correct drift in existing root README.md and root AGENTS.md: any stale references to file paths, section names, or structure that have since moved (notably content relocated from CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md to rules/).
- Verify README.md Skills table and Agents section against the actual contents of src/user/.agents/skills/ and src/user/.agents/agents/.
- Delete the 4-line stub at src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md in the same PR that adds src/user/.claude/README.md (README added first, stub removed after).

## Out of scope
- .github/ scaffolding (issue templates, PR templates, workflows)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- Restructuring or rewriting the root README for style
- Changes to docs/plans/ or docs/specs/ content
- Changes to src/plugins/AGENTS.md (already in good shape) — but root README tree SHOULD mention src/plugins/
- Any non-doc changes (no code edits, no install.sh changes)
- Any edits inside the root AGENTS.md BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 marker block — auto-generated by the beads plugin, regenerated on bd runs. Only the non-generated Session Completion and Beads Quick Reference blocks that appear outside or alongside the markers should be deduplicated.

## Design notes

### Drift detection (README.md / AGENTS.md)

Concrete checks — after edits, these greps should return nothing (or only current-truth results):

- No instruction to "copy CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template" without also mentioning rules/.
- No claim that `<orchestration>`, `<delegation>`, `<completion-gate>`, `<delivery>`, or `<beads>` sections live in CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md. They live in INSTRUCTIONS.md (orchestration) and rules/ (the rest).
- No reference to a skill name that doesn't correspond to a directory in src/user/.agents/skills/.
- No reference to an agent name that doesn't correspond to a file in src/user/.agents/agents/.

### Skills/Agents verification

Bidirectional by directory name:
- Every directory in src/user/.agents/skills/ has a row in the README Skills table.
- Every row in the README Skills table corresponds to a real directory.
- Agents section either lists the shipping agent names or points to the directory; do NOT leave it as vague prose.

### Beads integration markers

Root AGENTS.md contains `<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 ... -->` and `<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->` markers around a plugin-managed block. This block is regenerated by the beads plugin on install and must not be edited by hand. Any deduplication work on AGENTS.md must target content OUTSIDE these markers.

### Subfolder README style

- Short (roughly 20-50 lines each). Short enough that a first-time visitor can scan.
- Link up to the root README for install flow; do not duplicate install instructions.
- Answer three questions per folder: what lives here, how does it get installed, who is it for.

### LICENSE

Canonical MIT license text. Format:
```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2026 Scott Hamilton

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, ...
```

### CONTRIBUTING.md

Example draft:
> This is currently a personal configuration repository. If you find it useful and want to contribute agents, skills, or commands, please open an issue to discuss before sending a PR. See AGENTS.md for file format conventions (agent frontmatter, skill SKILL.md layout, command markdown).

### Delivery

- Single branch, single PR.
- Docs-only. No code changes, no install.sh changes, no settings changes.
- Drift fixes are surgical — edit only stale claims, not prose style.

## Open questions
- None blocking. Minor copy-edit decisions during implementation are fine to decide inline.

title: Audit and prune unused skills, agents, and commands
description:
The agents-config repo has accumulated a large inventory of skills, agents, and slash commands across src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, and src/plugins/. Some of these are actively used, others have never been invoked in practice. This audit-and-prune task identifies which are carrying their weight and which are dead weight.

## Why this exists
Over time, skills/agents/commands get added speculatively or for one-off needs and then never revisited. Every extra entry in the inventory is cognitive overhead for the agent at selection time and maintenance burden for the user. Pruning unused entries keeps the configuration sharp and the agent's attention focused on the tools that actually pay rent.

## What needs to be done
- Inventory all skills (src/user/.agents/skills/, src/plugins/*/skills/), agents (src/user/.agents/agents/), and commands (src/user/.claude/commands/, src/plugins/*/commands/) currently installed
- Gather usage evidence: grep transcripts/session logs if available, ask the user which ones they recognize using, cross-reference against recent PRs and commits for skills invoked
- Categorize each entry:
  - KEEP — actively used, clearly valuable
  - PRUNE — never used, redundant, or superseded
  - UNCERTAIN — escalate to user for judgment
- For PRUNE candidates: confirm with user, then remove from the repo
- For UNCERTAIN: present a short summary (name + description + last-seen-used signal) and let the user decide
- Update any references (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill indexes) after removals

## Notes
- This is a capture bead — brainstorm/spec work happens later when it's picked up
- Scope excludes the plugin-provided inventories (beads plugin, superpowers plugin, codex plugin, etc.) — those are managed upstream
- Consider whether a recurring lightweight audit (quarterly?) would be useful as a follow-on

title: Discovered-work snippets use <placeholder> inside bash if-condition — invalid shell if copy/pasted
design: Small-scope follow-up: three near-identical doc edits. Fits a single worktree + PR. Same methodology as 9cz (worktree → fix → code-reviewer + simplifier → PR → Copilot → merge-and-cleanup).
notes:
# Problem

In PR #13 (now merged as db41812), Copilot's second review round flagged 3 inline snippets where the discovered-work decision uses:

    if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && <work-is-sibling-subtask-of-$PARENT>; then
      bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --parent "$PARENT"
    else
      NEW=$(bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --json | jq -r '.id')
      bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
    fi

The `<work-is-sibling-subtask-of-$PARENT>` token is a placeholder, but in bash boolean context `<` is parsed as input redirect — so literal copy-paste would try to open a file. Unlike `<type>`/`<prio>` (placeholders inside string args, safe), the if-condition placeholder is syntactically dangerous.

Copilot's suggestion: introduce an `IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=true/false` variable (or split the if into two separate command sequences), so the snippet is runnable-looking even if placeholder values haven't been substituted.

# Affected files

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md line 77
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml line 319 (housekeeping step 4)
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml line 314 (housekeeping step 4)

# Copilot comment IDs

- 3107495666 (create-bead)
- 3107495677 (implement-feature)
- 3107495680 (fix-bug)

# Acceptance

- All three if-condition snippets replaced with shell-valid form (commented `IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK` boolean, or split into two explicit command paths).
- No more `<something-with-bash-chars>` placeholders inside boolean/expression contexts.
- Reply to each of the 3 Copilot comments pointing at the fix commit; resolve the 3 threads on the new PR.
- TOML still parses.

# Discovered-from

Discovered during wait-for-pr-comments round 2 on PR #13 (agents-config-9cz). Originating bead has no parent epic, so this is orphan + discovered-from (not an epic sibling).
